title: Rabdosia adenoloma
taxonomy: Rabdosia adenoloma is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Rabdosia, which is a group of plants commonly known as Himalayan sage or Himalayan mint. Rabdosia adenoloma is closely related to other plants in the Lamiaceae family, such as the genus Mentha and the genus Nepeta. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Rabdosia adenoloma, also known as Yunnan corydalis or Yunnan prince's corydalis, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Rabdosia adenoloma include alkaloids, such as adenolomine and adenolol, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
